heading: Intelligent Research Assistant using RAG and LLMs
text: Intelligent Research Assistant using RAG and LLMs Objective 1: User Authentication and Personal Workspace Module 1. Title “Design and Development of a Secure User Authentication and Personal Workspace Management System for an Intelligent Research Assistant Platform” 2. Background Modern AI platforms require secure and personalized access mechanisms to: • protect user data, • maintain conversation history, • manage uploaded research documents, • support multi-user environments. In an Intelligent Research Assistant system, multiple users may: • upload research papers, • interact with AI agents, • store conversations, • manage projects, • access personalized research workspaces. Therefore, a secure authentication and session management system is essential. 3. Problem Statement Most beginner AI applications: • do not support secure authentication, • lack proper user management, • cannot maintain personalized sessions, • expose user data insecurely,

heading: • fail to support scalable multi-user interaction.
text: • fail to support scalable multi-user interaction. The challenge is to design and implement a secure, scalable, and user-friendly authentication system that supports: • user registration, • login, • session management, • protected APIs, • personalized workspaces, • secure access to AI services. 4. Objective The objective is to develop a secure authentication and workspace management system for the Intelligent Research Assistant platform that enables users to: • Register securely • Login using credentials or OAuth • Maintain authenticated sessions • Access their personal dashboard • Store research documents and chat history securely while following modern web security practices. 5. System Goals The system should provide: Functional Goals User Registration Allow new users to create accounts. User Login Authenticate users securely. Session Management Maintain secure login sessions.

heading: Personal Workspace
text: Personal Workspace Provide user-specific dashboards. Protected Routes Restrict unauthorized access. Logout Terminate user sessions securely. Security Goals • Password hashing • Token-based authentication • Session expiration • Secure API communication • Role-based access (optional) 6. Learning Objectives for Students Backend Development • REST APIs • API security • Middleware Authentication Systems • JWT tokens • OAuth • Session handling Database Design • User schema

heading: • Relational database management
text: • Relational database management Security Concepts • Password hashing • Authentication flow • Token expiration • Secure storage Deployment Concepts • Environment variables • Cloud authentication • API hosting 7. Functional Requirements Module 1 — User Registration Features • Signup form • Email validation • Password validation • Duplicate account prevention Input • Name • Email • Password Output • Successful account creation • Error handling

heading: Module 2 — User Login
text: Module 2 — User Login Features • Login using email/password • JWT token generation • Session creation Output • Access token • User authentication Module 3 — JWT Authentication Features • Token creation • Token validation • Token expiration • Protected APIs Module 4 — OAuth Login Features Login using: • Google • GitHub Learning • Third-party authentication Module 5 — Session Management Features • Persistent login

heading: • Secure logout
text: • Secure logout • Session timeout Module 6 — Personal Workspace Features User-specific: • Dashboard • Uploaded documents • Chat history • Saved summaries Module 7 — Authorization Features Restrict: • unauthorized document access • unauthorized API access 8. Non-Functional Requirements Security • Password encryption • HTTPS-ready APIs Scalability Support multiple users. Performance Fast authentication response.

heading: Reliability
text: Reliability Stable session handling. Usability Simple and intuitive interface. 9. Recommended Technology Stack Backend Options Beginner Friendly Flask OR FastAPI (Recommended) Why FastAPI? • Easy syntax • Async support • Swagger API docs • Better modern architecture Advanced Option Spring Boot Suitable for: • Java backend students Database PostgreSQL Used for:

heading: • user data
text: • user data • sessions • workspace metadata Authentication JWT Primary authentication method. Optional OAuth Firebase/Auth0 For: • Google login • social authentication Frontend (Optional UI Team) • React • HTML/CSS/JS • Tailwind CSS 10. High-Level System Design Authentication Flow User ↓ Register/Login API ↓ Backend Authentication Service ↓ PostgreSQL Database ↓ JWT Token Generated ↓

heading: Frontend Stores Token
text: Frontend Stores Token ↓ Protected API Access 11. Database Design Users Table Field Type id Integer name String email String password_hash String created_at Timestamp Sessions Table (Optional) Field Type session_id String user_id Integer login_time Timestamp expiry_time Timestamp Workspace Table Field Type workspace_id Integer user_id Integer project_name String created_at Timestamp

heading: 12. API Design
text: 12. API Design Authentication APIs Register API POST /register Input: { "name":"John", "email":"john@gmail.com", "password":"123456" } Login API POST /login Output: { "access_token":"JWT_TOKEN" } Protected User API GET /profile Requires: Authorization: Bearer TOKEN Logout API POST /logout 13. Step-by-Step Development Plan

heading: Phase 1 — Environment Setup
text: Phase 1 — Environment Setup Students install: • Python • FastAPI • PostgreSQL • VS Code • Postman Phase 2 — Database Setup Tasks: • Create PostgreSQL database • Design user schema Phase 3 — User Registration Students implement: • form validation • password hashing Phase 4 — Login System Students implement: • credential verification • JWT generation Phase 5 — Protected APIs Students learn: • middleware • token validation

heading: Phase 6 — Session Management
text: Phase 6 — Session Management Tasks: • persistent sessions • logout system Phase 7 — Personal Workspace Students build: • dashboard • user profile • workspace page Phase 8 — OAuth Integration (Optional) Students integrate: • Google login Phase 9 — Deployment Deploy: • backend server • database • frontend 14. Security Concepts Students Must Learn Password Hashing Use: • bcrypt Never store raw passwords.

heading: JWT Security
text: JWT Security Understand: • token expiration • refresh tokens API Security Learn: • authentication middleware • authorization Environment Variables Store: • secret keys • database credentials 15. Expected Final Deliverables Students should submit: 1. Source Code GitHub repository with: • clean structure • documentation 2. API Documentation Swagger/Postman collection. 3. Working Authentication System Features:

heading: • register
text: • register • login • JWT • dashboard 4. Technical Report Include: • architecture • database design • API workflow 5. Deployment Link Hosted backend/frontend.
